setup.title: Match Setup
setup.description: Configure your opponent and select formations to analyze
setup.name: Name

analysis.title: Analysis:
analysis.selectFormationHint: Select a formation to see detailed performance

percentileModal.title: Understanding Percentile Calculations
percentileModal.keyInsightText: Percentiles are calculated separately for each formation. Each formation's matches are compared only against other matches using that same formation.
percentileModal.whyHigherLower: Why Higher Values Can Have Lower Percentiles
percentileModal.exampleNote: 3-5-2 has the lowest value but highest percentile because 304 is exceptional for a 3-5-2!
percentileModal.tacticalNote: More tactically useful than comparing raw numbers across different systems.

guideModal.title: Formation Fluidity Guide
guideModal.slide0Intro: Formation labels (4-2-3-1, 3-5-2, etc.) are SNAPSHOTS of fluid tactical systems.
guideModal.slide0Item1: Teams constantly shift between shapes based on phase of play
guideModal.slide0Item2: Player positioning and movement create dynamic variations
guideModal.slide0Item3: Tactical instructions adapt formations in real-time
guideModal.slide0Item4: Game situation and scoreline influence shape changes
guideModal.slide0Highlight: This tool helps you understand which formations perform best against specific opponents.
guideModal.slide1Intro: A common transformation during possession phase:
guideModal.slide2Intro: RB inverts to midfield, LB pushes high as wide attacker:
guideModal.slide3OverloadDesc: Areas where you have MORE players than the opponent.
guideModal.slide3OverloadExample: 4-3-3 vs 4-2-3-1: 3 CMs vs 2 CDMs = 3v2 midfield advantage
guideModal.slide3UnderloadDesc: Areas where you have FEWER players than the opponent.
guideModal.slide3UnderloadExample: 4-3-3 vs 4-4-2: 1 ST vs 2 CBs = isolated striker
guideModal.slide3Highlight: Use this tool to find formations that create overloads where you need them!
guideModal.slide4TipText: Hover over metric headers to see explanations!
guideModal.slide5Step1Desc: Choose the formation and tier of your next opponent
guideModal.slide5Step3Desc: Pick formations you're considering to compare
guideModal.slide6Item2: Percentiles show performance relative to that formation
guideModal.slide6Item4: Compare multiple formations to find the best fit
guideModal.slide6Item5: Use Focus Mode to analyze one formation in depth
guideModal.slide6FinalNote:
The tool provides the framework.
You bring the tactical creativity.

gauge.invertedNote: Lower values are better for this metric

insights.noExtremes: No extreme values in this category - all formations perform within normal range.

report.bestChoice: {formation} is the best choice against this opponent with a {winRate}% win rate across {matches} matches.
report.higherWinRateText: {formation} has a higher win rate ({winRate}%) but with only {matches} matches - less statistically reliable.
report.lowSampleWarning: The following formations have limited data (n<20):
report.resultsNotSignificant: Results may not be statistically significant.
report.tacticalText: Against this opponent configuration, your formations average {possession}% possession and {xg} xG per match.
report.dominatePossession: Your formations tend to dominate possession.
report.counterAttack: Expect to play more on the counter-attack.
report.balancedPossession: Possession is typically balanced in these matchups.

errors.checkFile: Make sure the JSON file is in the same directory
errors.noFormations: No formations found with data against this opponent configuration
errors.selectAtLeastOne: Please select at least one formation to analyze
errors.noData: No data available for this category
